ContentType.description:
Closed vocabulary for ``signals.content_type``.

A closed enum means a consumer's filter never silently misses a typo'd or
unexpected variant; an unfamiliar page is classified as :attr:`OTHER` rather
than inventing a new label. See ``docs/data-model.md``.
ContentType.title: ContentType

Signals.description:
Derived quality signals, grouped so they can be versioned as one unit.

Nesting these under ``signals`` is a contract choice, not a performance one:
each JSONL record loads whole, so the grouping simply keeps identity fields
separate from the derived "filter panel".

is_mostly_code.description: Whether the page is predominantly code.
is_mostly_code.title: Is Mostly Code

language.description: ISO 639-1 language code, or 'und' when detection is not possible.
language.title: Language

Signals.title: Signals

description:
One kept web page, serialized as one line of JSONL.

The shape is *hybrid*: identity and extracted-content fields live at the top
level, while derived signals are grouped under :class:`Signals`. Collections
default to empty (never null); genuinely optional scalars default to null.

content_hash.description: sha256 hex of body_text; drives change detection.
content_hash.title: Content Hash

extra.description: Optional structured attributes (e.g. from JSON-LD); {} if none.
extra.title: Extra

fetched_at.description: When the current version was captured; tz-aware UTC ISO8601.
fetched_at.title: Fetched At

id.description: uuid5 of the canonical URL; stable identity / upsert key.
id.title: Id

published_at.description: Publication timestamp (ISO8601 UTC) or null if none exists.
published_at.title: Published At

title.description: Page title resolved by a precedence cascade; '' if none.
title.title: Title

url.description: The canonical URL of the page (the exact string hashed for id).
url.title: Url

title: Document
